history.label: 2nd century
history.text: Ulpiana was an important Roman city on the Balkan Peninsula and in the 2nd century BC it was declared a municipium.

history.label: 4th century
history.text: During the 4th century BC, the Kingdom of Dardania was established in the region.

history.label: 8th century
history.text: In the area of Pristina, a hilltop settlement appears since the 8th century BCE at an elevation of 685 metres near the village Teneshdoll, around 16 kilometres north of the Pristina city center.

history.label: 12th century
history.text: Between the late 11th and middle of the 12th century it was ceded several times to the Second Bulgarian Empire, before being annexed by the Kingdom (later Empire) of Serbia, which held it under.
In 1315, the nearby Gračanica monastery was founded by Serbian King Stefan Milutin.

After the League of Prizren (1878), the Ottoman authorities moved the capital of the vilayet of Kosovo to Prishtina, likely to weaken the influence of the League of Prizren and to strengthen their control.

history.label: 20th century
history.text: The first registered state printing press was moved to the city, and key buildings like today’s Kosovo Museum (completed in 1885) were built. 20th century In May 1901, Albanians pillaged and partially burned Pristina.
In late October 1918, the 11th French colonial division took over Pristina and returned Pristina back to what then became the 'First Yugoslavia' on 1 December 1918.

Prishtina’s post-1999 urban growth has also been the subject of academic study, including research that examines how university campuses interact with broader city planning and urban transformation within the frameworks of sustainable and resilient.

history.label: 21st century
history.text: A majority of the city's 45,000 Serb inhabitants fled from Kosovo and today only several dozen remain in the city. 21st century Pristina International Airport's new terminal opened for operations in October 2013, which.
Another extensive development for the city has been the completion of the R6 motorway in 2019, connecting Pristina to North Macedonia's capital, Skopje.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The inhabitants of this city, most of whom are Albanians, call themselves Prishtinali in the local Gheg Albanian. Early development The area of Pristina has been inhabited since the Neolithic era by Early European.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: During the period between the two World Wars, Serb colonist families were settled both in the city itself and across many villages, newly established settlements or within existing villages.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: The Jews were later deported to Germany, where many were killed.This ended a long period when the institution had been run as an outpost of Belgrade University and gave a major boost to Albanian-language.
